SVG data table titled 'Student's table of processed data' with columns Drop height (h1) | Average height of first bounce (h2) / m | Coefficient of restitution (e). Rows: 50.0 cm | 0.34 | 0.82; 2.50 m | 1.44 | 0.76; 1.50 m | 0.94 | 0.74; 2.00 m | 1.21 | (blank for student to calculate); 1.00 m | 0.65 | 0.806. Inconsistent units and the missing e cell are reproduced exactly because the task asks the student to spot the errors and compute e for 2.00 m.
Mis-crop: 'Create New Table' / Reset UI, Question 5d hypothesis box and Question 5e statement box with the e = (speed after)/(speed before) = v2/v1 formula. Pure text/formula and UI, no diagram.

SVG: textbook illustration of a table tennis racket with a ball, showing the layered construction. Labels: 'Layer of wood' (wooden blade core), 'Rubber' (red top surface with pimple texture), 'Sponge' (cushioning layer between wood and rubber), 'Handle'. An exposed edge reveals the rubber / sponge / wood layers in cross-section.
Mis-crop: Question 6b text and 'Research question / Independent variable / Dependent variable (Height of the first bounce)' empty answer boxes. Pure text and form fields, no diagram.

Mis-crop: 'Control variable 1 / Control variable 2' empty boxes (end of Q6b) and Question 7 intro text with a 'Video / Script' transcript about passively heated houses. Pure text and form fields, no diagram.

render_data.title: Passive house — match each feature to how it reduces heat loss
render_data.prompt: A passively heated house uses six key features to stay warm with less energy. Match each feature to the physics it relies on.
items: Thermal insulation of walls and roof
Windows with three layers of glass
bins: Prevents air moving between inside and outside (reduces convection)
Mechanical ventilation circulates warm air
Does not easily transfer heat (reduces conduction)
Minimizes thermal contact with the ground
Positioned to maximize solar heat entering
Sealed, coated panes let sunlight in but prevent radiation leaving

correct.Airtightness: Prevents air moving between inside and outside (reduces convection)
correct.Thermal insulation of walls and roof: Does not easily transfer heat (reduces conduction)
correct.Insulated floor slab: Minimizes thermal contact with the ground
correct.Direction of windows: Positioned to maximize solar heat entering
correct.Windows with three layers of glass: Sealed, coated panes let sunlight in but prevent radiation leaving

image_prompt: Mis-crop: Question 7a and 7b prompts with empty rich-text answer boxes. Pure text and UI, no diagram.

SVG stacked bar chart 'Household energy consumption for heating by energy', y-axis Percentage / % 0-100 (ticks every 10), single stacked bar of sources of energy used for heating. Segments bottom-to-top: Coal ~2%, Electricity ~5% (2-7), Heat ~8% (7-15), Wood ~22% (15-37), Oil ~15% (37-52), Gas ~48% (52-100). Used to read off the percentage of households using oil (~15%). This page shows the partial top of the same chart; replaced by the full chart SVG.

SVG stacked bar chart 'Household energy consumption for heating by energy', y-axis Percentage / % 0-100 (ticks every 10), single stacked bar of sources of energy used for heating. Segments bottom-to-top: Coal ~2%, Electricity ~5% (2-7), Heat ~8% (7-15), Wood ~22% (15-37), Oil ~15% (37-52), Gas ~48% (52-100). The student reads the percentage of households using oil (~15%) off this chart.

Infographic slide 'Cost comparison of actively heated and passively heated buildings' — bulleted text with a money-bag clip-art. All numeric content (€180,000 / €195,000 build, 90% / €822 saving, €15,000 upgrade) is already in the question stem; the figure is decorative text.
Infographic slides 'Regulating the passive house standard' (clipboard clip-art) and 'Paying for heat' (gauge clip-art) — bulleted text already captured in the question stem; decorative.

Infographic slide 'Air quality in passively heated houses' (smog cloud clip-art) plus the Question 8 discuss/evaluate prompt and empty answer box. Bulleted text already captured in the question stem; decorative.
